title: Fluffy Tunisian Oats Pilaf
description: A Tunisian-inspired breakfast pilaf made with rolled oats, vegetables, and a hint of spice. It's a great twist on traditional oatmeal, perfect for a quick and flavorful breakfast.

ingredients.name: rolled oats
ingredients.notes: any type of oats will work, but old-fashioned or rolled oats are best for this recipe

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the grated carrots, sliced zucchini, and sliced red bell pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the oats, stir to combine with the vegetables, and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the oats are lightly toasted.
Push the oats and vegetables to one side of the pan.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble them until they're cooked through, breaking them up into small curds.
Mix the eggs with the oats and vegetables.
Serve the Fluffy Tunisian Oats Pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
